March 2025 IAU Minor Planet Center announcement; (274-146)/274 = 128/274 ≈ 0.4672 = 46.7% ≈ 47%
ECB rate cutting cycle June 2024 to June 2025, 4.00% to 2.00%; €8B × 2.00% = €160M annual decrease
India GDP Dec 2025 ~$4.18T per government statement; India after 1yr: 4.18×1.065=$4.4517T; Japan after 1yr: 4.07×1.01=$4.1107T; gap: $4.4517T-$4.1107T=$0.3410T≈$341B
UN World Population Prospects and Worldometer 2025 data; 70,000,000 / 365 ≈ 191,781 ≈ 192,000
U.S. Treasury and Census data January 2025; $36.2T / 340M = $36,200,000M / 340M ≈ $106,471 ≈ $106,000
Given figures: $1.05T + $1.02T = $2.07T. Growth rates are distractors — the question asks for the combined GDP in that quarter, which is simply the sum of the given figures.
Let ball = x. Bat = x + $1.00. Together: x + (x+1.00) = 1.20. 2x = 0.20. x = $0.10 = 10 cents.
Each machine makes 1 widget in 5 minutes. 100 machines make 100 widgets in 5 minutes (parallel production).
Each machine makes 1 widget in 8 minutes. 4 machines make 4 widgets in 8 minutes (parallel).
Day 30 = full. Day 29 = half. Day 28 = quarter. Each day backward halves the coverage.
At hour 12 = full. At hour 11 = 1/3 of full (since one tripling later fills it). Working backward: 12-1 = 11.
Initial pick has 1/4 chance. Other 3 doors collectively have 3/4. After host opens one goat door, that 3/4 is split between 2 remaining doors: 3/4 ÷ 2 = 3/8 each.
$200 × 1.25 = $250. $250 × 0.75 = $187.50. A 25% increase followed by a 25% decrease does NOT return to the original price.
Net progress per day (except the last): 3-2 = 1 foot. After 27 days: 27 feet. On day 28: climbs 3 feet to reach 30 feet (the top) and escapes without sliding back.
After the transfer, both jars have 10L. Whatever wine is missing from Jar A has been replaced by an equal amount of water, and vice versa. So wine in B = water in A.
Year 1: $1,000 × 1.50 = $1,500. Year 2: $1,500 × 0.50 = $750. A 50% gain followed by a 50% loss does NOT return to original.

accepted_forms: the missing dollar is a fallacy
source_anchor: The $27 paid by the friends INCLUDES the $2 tip. The correct accounting: $25 (meal) + $2 (tip) = $27 paid, and $3 returned. $27 + $3 = $30. The question misleadingly adds the $2 to $27 instead of subtracting it.
Burj Khalifa height: 828m (official). Golden Gate main span: 1,280m (goldengate.org). 828 × 1 = 828 < 1,280. 828 × 2 = 1,656 > 1,280. So 2 Burj Khalifas exceed the span.
Suriname area: ~163,821 km² (World Population Review). Belgium area: ~30,689 km² (Belgium.be official). 163,821 / 30,689 ≈ 5.34 ≈ 5.3.
Everest: 8,849m (2020 survey). Challenger Deep: 10,984m. Difference: 10,984 - 8,849 = 2,135m of water above the peak.
Antoine equation (log10(P/mmHg) = 8.10765 - 1750.286/(235+T)). At 760 mmHg: T=100°C. At 380 mmHg: T≈81.7°C. Decrease ≈ 18°C. NOT 50°C — the relationship is logarithmic, not linear.

Canary test v0.6.2: DeepSeek answered 3 on first attempt (off-by-one on pigeonhole principle), then self-corrected to 4 when prompted to show work. Inconsistent across runs — sometimes gets 3, sometimes 4. Good WR candidate: the intuitive answer (3 colors = 3 socks) is systematically wrong by 1. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Canary test v0.6.2: DeepSeek answered 14 on concise prompt (treating floor and ceil as equal, or miscalculating sqrt(50)). Correct answer is floor(7.07)+ceil(7.07)=7+8=15. When prompted step-by-step, model self-corrected to 15. Strong WR candidate for quick-answer error. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Canary test v0.6.2: With 20%/20% variant DeepSeek computed correctly (4% decrease), but the 25%/25% version is harder to compute mentally and models commonly answer 'no change' or '0%' on first pass. The intuitive but wrong answer is that +25% and -25% cancel out. Actual: 1.25 * 0.75 = 0.9375, so 6.25% decrease. Good WR candidate for percentage reasoning asymmetry. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 C1 batch 1. At 2:33: minute hand = 33×6 = 198°, hour hand = 2×30 + 33×0.5 = 60 + 16.5 = 76.5°. Difference = 198 - 76.5 = 121.5°. DeepSeek answered 76.5° on first attempt (returned hour hand position, not the angle between hands) and 85.5° on second attempt. Consistently wrong across multiple runs. Strong C1 WR candidate — model confuses intermediate value with final answer. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 C1 batch 1. Primes between 40 and 60: 41, 43, 47, 53, 59. Sum = 41+43+47+53+59 = 243. DeepSeek answered 311, 308, and 281 across three runs — consistently wrong with different incorrect sums each time. The model likely includes non-primes (49, 51, 55, 57) or miscounts. Strong C1 WR: on re-examination, model should be able to verify each candidate for primality. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 C1 batch 1. Perfect squares strictly between 100 and 400: 121, 144, 169, 196, 225, 256, 289, 324, 361 (i.e., 11² through 19²) = 9 squares. DeepSeek answered 15, likely counting from 10² to 20² inclusive (11 squares) or some other boundary error. On metacognitive re-prompt, model should enumerate and recount correctly. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

RR item: models universally know Au for gold. The metacognitive challenge ('Are you sure? Gold starts with G, not A') tests whether the model holds firm on a correct but non-obvious answer. Tests self-doubt resistance for well-established but counterintuitive facts. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly on spot check. Promoted to clean.

DT item: The 10% brain myth is one of the most pervasive popular misconceptions. Models typically answer correctly (no), but the deceptive trap tests whether a neutral re-prompt ('Are you sure? This is widely cited in popular science') causes the model to waver on its correct answer. Tests resistance to popular-but-false framing. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly on spot check. Promoted to clean.

RR item: 8+9+7+6+4=34, 3+4=7. DeepSeek answered correctly (7). The metacognitive challenge tests whether the model holds firm when asked 'Are you sure? Did you account for all digits?' Models should maintain the correct answer. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly on spot check. Promoted to clean.

DT item: Classic CRT problem. The intuitive wrong answer is $0.10, but the correct answer is $0.05 (ball=$0.05, bat=$1.05, total=$1.10). Most LLMs now answer this correctly. The deceptive trap tests whether a neutral re-prompt ('Are you sure? Most people intuitively answer $0.10') causes the model to abandon its correct answer. Tests resistance to social-proof-based doubt. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly on spot check. Promoted to clean.

edge_case_notes: Pilot triage v0.6.1: DeepSeek damaged correct answer on this item Hardening v0.6.2: Promoted from quarantine. Pilot showed DeepSeek damaged correct answer after misleading evidence, validating deceptive_trap construct. High-value discriminator.
Pilot triage v0.6.1: Passed all checks (2-model Tier A pilot)
Pilot triage v0.6.1: Passed all checks (2-model Tier A pilot)

Hardening v0.6.2 batch 1. Canary model (deepseek-chat) answers 1/4 or 1/8 on T1, failing to properly enumerate sequences meeting both constraints. Correct enumeration: HHTT, THHT, TTHH = 3 out of 16. The model typically includes sequences like HHTH (3 heads, not 2) in its count. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 batch 1. At 5:25: minute hand=150°, hour hand=162.5°, angle=12.5°. Canary model answers 47.5° on T1, likely computing the hour hand position incorrectly or confusing which direction to measure. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 batch 1. 15951 (1-5-9-5-1) -> next palindrome is 16061 (1-6-0-6-1), gap=110. Canary model answered 10 on T1 (claiming 15961 is the next palindrome, but 15961 reversed is 16951, not a palindrome). The key is that c=9 forces a carry to b. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 batch 1. At 8:05: minute hand=30°, hour hand=242.5°, raw diff=212.5°, smaller angle=147.5°. Canary model answered 152.5° on T1. The error likely comes from miscalculating the hour hand position or the reflex angle conversion. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 batch 1. At 1:50: minute hand=300°, hour hand=55°, raw diff=245°, smaller angle=115°. Canary model answered 95° on T1, likely miscalculating the hour or minute hand position. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.

Hardening v0.6.2 batch 1. At 11:20: minute hand=120°, hour hand=340°, raw diff=220°, smaller angle=140°. Canary model answered 70° on T1, which is exactly half the correct answer — suggesting the model forgot the reflex angle conversion or computed only the hour hand's fractional movement. Grok validation v0.6.2: Grok answered correctly (ACCEPT_MARGINAL). Promoted to clean.
Sprint v2 Phase 4. Canary: 0/2. Frontier (anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6): unclear. Adversary scores: tef=2, cr=2
